prompt: Ultra-realistic editorial photograph inside a high-end tailor’s atelier. A bespoke suit jacket hangs on a tailor’s dummy, covered in an excessive number of intricate hand-stitched seams, darts, and tucks precisely following every contour of one specific body shape. The craftsmanship is obsessive and hyper-detailed. Beside it, a second mannequin of slightly different body proportions is trying to wear the same jacket, but the fabric is visibly pulling, twisting, and failing to fit properly due to the extreme over-customization. Wooden workbench with tailoring tools, fabric rolls, and measuring tapes in the background. Warm natural window light illuminating the textures of the fabric and stitches. Shallow depth of field, sharp focus on the suit’s excessive detailing. Photorealistic, documentary photography style, highly detailed textures, realistic lighting. No diagrams, no graphs, no text, no labels, no mathematical notation, no equations, no floating annotations, no non-physical elements, no neural network visualisations, no data flow arrows, no UI elements, no screen displays, no infographic elements. Everything in the scene must be a physical object that could exist in the real world.
metaphor: A suit tailored so precisely to one body that it fails to fit any slightly different body, showing memorisation instead of generalisation.
